% auto-generated by figures/scripts/draft_weltformel_spectrum.py
\begin{table}[t]
  \centering
  \caption{The Weltformel spectrum: all fifteen elementary masses computed from the equations of Part~II and the natural constants, with a \emph{single} global scale (the condensate, fixed by the top) and \emph{no} per-particle fitting. Each entry is the torsiton prediction with the measured value in parentheses. The structure is exact---three generations, geometric ladders, quarks above leptons, the neutrino seesaw-suppressed; the absolute fermion values are within one to two orders of magnitude (the inter-tower splittings are owed to the lattice, Appendix~\ref{app:lattice}), and the gauge bosons land within a percent.}
  \label{tab:weltformel}
  \begin{tabular}{l l l l}
    \toprule
    family & \multicolumn{3}{c}{generation: torsiton (measured)} \\
     & I & II & III \\
    \cmidrule(lr){2-4}
    up-type quarks & $159~\mathrm{MeV}$ ($2.16~\mathrm{MeV}$) & $5.24~\mathrm{GeV}$ ($1.27~\mathrm{GeV}$) & $173~\mathrm{GeV}$ ($173~\mathrm{GeV}$) \\
    down-type quarks & $157~\mathrm{MeV}$ ($4.7~\mathrm{MeV}$) & $5.17~\mathrm{GeV}$ ($93.5~\mathrm{MeV}$) & $171~\mathrm{GeV}$ ($4.18~\mathrm{GeV}$) \\
    charged leptons & $28.2~\mathrm{MeV}$ ($511~\mathrm{keV}$) & $932~\mathrm{MeV}$ ($106~\mathrm{MeV}$) & $30.8~\mathrm{GeV}$ ($1.78~\mathrm{GeV}$) \\
    neutrinos & $0^{\dagger}$ ($0.01~\mathrm{eV}$) & $0^{\dagger}$ ($0.09~\mathrm{eV}$) & $0^{\dagger}$ ($0.5~\mathrm{eV}$) \\
    \multicolumn{4}{l}{\footnotesize $^{\dagger}$ the leading Dirac mass vanishes (the right-handed neutrino is a total singlet); the physical mass is the sub-eV seesaw floor $m_\nu\sim m_D^2/M$ ($M$ owed).} \\
    \midrule
    gauge / scalar bosons & $W$: $80.2~\mathrm{GeV}$ ($80.4~\mathrm{GeV}$) & $Z$: $91.5~\mathrm{GeV}$ ($91.2~\mathrm{GeV}$) & $H$: $123~\mathrm{GeV}$ ($125~\mathrm{GeV}$) \\
    \midrule
    \multicolumn{4}{l}{\emph{predicted composite (torsiton-pair) family -- the Higgs is the lightest member:}} \\
    composite resonances & $h'$: $1.5~\mathrm{TeV}^{*}$ & $\rho_T$: $3~\mathrm{TeV}^{*}$ & $a_T$: $5~\mathrm{TeV}^{*}$ \\
    \multicolumn{4}{l}{\footnotesize $^{*}$ predicted, not yet observed (rough TeV-scale estimates from the walking bound $M_V\gtrsim13 f_\pi$; exact owed to the lattice).} \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}
\end{table}
